rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEANDRUS BENTON SR, DECEASED SEPTEMBER 9, 2010
rulings.Calendar Matter: First American Title Companys Notice Of Motion For Discharge Pursuant To Ccp Sections 386 And 386.6
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Hon. Joseph M. Quinn: First American Title Company's motion for discharge pursuant to CCP Sections 386 and 386.6, which includes a request for fees and costs to be awarded out of the interpleader funds on deposit, is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. First American is discharged from liability and dismissed from this action under CCP § 386.5, in light of the June 24, 2019 deposit of the interpleaded funds. First American's requests for fees and costs is statutorily authorized. The court has previously decided that the interpleader action is appropriate and the court will not revisit that decision. The court in its discretion finds that a fees and costs award is appropriate in this matter. First American Title Company is awarded $510 in costs ($60 for the Motion for Discharge; $450 for the Petition in Interpleader). First American is awarded $3,290 in reasonable attorney's fees, incurred in pursuit of the Petition for Interpleader and this Motion for Discharge Pursuant to CCP §§386 and 386.6. The court's calculation of reasonable fees is as follows: 5.4 hours spent drafting the Petition, at $220 per hour; 6 hours spent drafting this Motion, at $260 per hour; two hours for the Reply and anticipated appearance at the hearing, at $260 per hour. To the extent First American seeks additional fees and costs, the request is denied. The total amount of $3,800 shall be awarded out of the interpleader funds on deposit with the court in this matter. The clerk shall release said sum to First American from the funds held by the court on deposit in this matter. First American to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, July 24, 2019.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Reconsider Courts Order Granting Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action Lis Pendens Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to Reconsider is denied for each of three independent reasons. First, Petitioner's moving papers did not include the declaration required by Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(b) setting out the new or different facts, circumstances, or law on which the motion must be based. Second, citing cases over 80 years old for propositions addressed in the original motion does not constitute new or different facts, circumstances, or law for purposes of section 1008. These cases could have been included in the original briefing. Third, even if the Court were to reach the merits a second time, the result would not change. None of the cases now cited involved a determination of whether a petition for probate constituted a real property action sufficient to support a lis pendens where the subject real property is titled to a trust (albeit one whose validity is challenged) rather than in decedent's name. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, July 24, 2019.
